response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and web-platform engineer whose career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. His C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 working on a C IDE, with no subsequent C++ roles — and is far below the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development the role mandates. There is no evidence of Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience; his sole finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) involved software-craftsmanship practices for a German investment-product platform, not capital-markets systems. SQL is listed as a skill but SQL Server and Oracle are absent, and the resume shows no exposure to Summit, Kafka/MQ, or STL. The candidate's genuine strengths lie in a domain entirely orthogonal to this engagement.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps discipline (GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development, Altom and RIDE Capital roles)
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record — multiple public workshops, conference talks, and open-source project leadership
Broad polyglot background including Java and some low-level systems work, indicating capacity to learn new environments
concerns: C++ experience is superficial (~6 months professionally in 2014–2015); the last decade has been exclusively web and TypeScript/Node.js work
Zero exposure to Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking systems — both domain tracks for the mandatory domain requirement are unmet
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL, which are not the platforms specified

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / Fixed Income domain knowledge
